capabilities.advertised_claim: Circle Calculus can state established zero-sum and prime-circle sumset theorems directly in the native `C_n` vocabulary.
capabilities.audience_interest: Erdos-Ginzburg-Ziv, Cauchy-Davenport, zero-sum witnesses, and finite sumsets.
capabilities.not_claimed: No new proof of EGZ, no new proof of Cauchy-Davenport, and no progress claim on an open Erdos problem.
capabilities.proof_scope: Lean-proved Circle wrappers around mathlib's EGZ and Cauchy-Davenport theorems.
capabilities.title: Zero-sum and sumset theorems on finite circles

capabilities.advertised_claim: Circle Calculus can expose a classical circle-method counting atom and the Erdos-Ko-Rado bound as proof-linked artifacts.
capabilities.audience_interest: Intersecting families, cyclic-order double counting, and sharp extremal examples.
capabilities.not_claimed: No new EKR proof and no classification of all extremal families.
capabilities.proof_scope: Lean-proved wrappers around mathlib's Katona prefix-density and Erdos-Ko-Rado declarations.
capabilities.title: Katona circle method and Erdos-Ko-Rado

capabilities.advertised_claim: Circle Calculus can connect finite witness searches to formal Roth-theorem handles.
capabilities.audience_interest: Dense finite sets, arithmetic progressions, Roth numbers, and witness search.
capabilities.not_claimed: No new Roth proof, no improved bounds, and no cyclic-to-natural transfer theorem yet.
capabilities.proof_scope: Lean-proved wrappers around mathlib's Roth theorem and Roth-number sublinearity statement.
capabilities.title: Roth and three-term arithmetic progressions

capabilities.advertised_claim: Circle Calculus can present Ramsey-style unavoidable structure with theorem ids and executable finite searches.
capabilities.audience_interest: Unavoidable monochromatic structure, combinatorial lines, and finite coloring examples.
capabilities.not_claimed: Finite Python searches are examples only; they are not the Hales-Jewett proof.
capabilities.proof_scope: Lean-proved wrappers around mathlib's Hales-Jewett and homothetic-copy theorems.
capabilities.title: Ramsey lines and Hales-Jewett structure

capabilities.advertised_claim: Circle Calculus can use cyclic graph models as a proof-backed bridge toward graph-geometry language.
capabilities.audience_interest: Cycle graphs, circulant graphs, connectedness, and unit-distance vocabulary.
capabilities.not_claimed: No progress on hard unit-distance or distinct-distance problems.
capabilities.proof_scope: Lean-proved cycle/circulant and connectedness bridges plus a unit-distance embedding constructor for empty graphs.
capabilities.title: Circulant graph and unit-distance graph lab

capabilities.advertised_claim: Circle Calculus can express detailed modular-arithmetic structure as period, orbit, kernel, and fiber theorems.
capabilities.audience_interest: GCD periods, kernels, fibers, image sizes, affine maps, and modular normal forms.
capabilities.not_claimed: No claim that this replaces standard modular arithmetic; it is a proof-carrying cyclic presentation.
capabilities.proof_scope: Lean-proved S1 scaling and orbit spine, including full-coil iff coprime and image-times-fiber factorizations.
capabilities.title: Factor, fiber, and period-normal modular arithmetic

capabilities.advertised_claim: Circle Calculus can express bounded quaternion and Hopf phase facts with explicit warnings about topology boundaries.
capabilities.audience_interest: Unit quaternions, Hopf coordinates, hidden phase, and sign ambiguity.
capabilities.not_claimed: No full smooth Hopf fibration formalization and no complete SO(3) robotics formalization.
capabilities.proof_scope: Lean-proved quaternion unit laws and bounded Hopf phase-action facts.
capabilities.title: Quaternion, Hopf, and phase coordinates

capabilities.advertised_claim: Circle Calculus can build a finite proof-carrying gauge-path model with endpoint and holonomy laws.
capabilities.audience_interest: Gauge links, holonomy, Wilson loops, plaquette invariance, and checked path composition.
capabilities.not_claimed: No continuum gauge theory, no physics discovery, and no lattice-QCD correctness claim.
capabilities.proof_scope: Lean-proved finite link-path, checked-path, gauge-transform, Wilson-loop, and plaquette facts.
capabilities.title: Proof-carrying finite gauge paths

capabilities.advertised_claim: Circle Calculus can pair generated structures with checked provenance and exact-comparison gates.
capabilities.audience_interest: Seed-rule records, exact regeneration, provenance, and generated diagram audit trails.
capabilities.not_claimed: No universal compression claim and no guarantee that generated artifacts are mathematically interesting.
capabilities.proof_scope: Lean-proved seed-rule, orbit-decomposition, proof-glyph, exact-regeneration, and negative-case gates.
capabilities.title: Proof-carrying generative provenance

capabilities.advertised_claim: Circle Calculus can prove finite indexing and schedule-safety facts that AI experiments can reuse.
capabilities.audience_interest: Cyclic memory, recurrence budgets, loop-exit certificates, phase routing, and adapter indexing.
capabilities.not_claimed: No model-quality, speed, context-length, or reasoning improvement claim without benchmark evidence.
capabilities.proof_scope: Lean-proved finite memory-slot, loop-budget, loop-exit, and adapter-block normalization facts.
capabilities.title: AI memory, loop, and adapter schedules

capabilities.advertised_claim: Circle Calculus can prove finite address-safety and schedule-normalization facts for cyclic computation.
capabilities.audience_interest: Cyclic buffers, stride layouts, periodic stencils, round-robin scheduling, and backend-safe rewrites.
capabilities.not_claimed: No performance claim without direct dense, FFT/NTT, and backend baselines.
capabilities.proof_scope: Lean-proved cyclic-address, stride-address, and round-robin schedule laws with Python layout fixtures.
capabilities.title: Circular compute layouts and schedules
